params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Colors data from Warmup. Red=40, Blue=30, Green=50, Yellow=20. Axis labeled 0 to 60 in intervals of 10. All 4 bars pre-filled.
Red bar highlights.
Horizontal guideline draws from top of Red bar to vertical axis at 40. The 40 mark on the axis glows.

beats.text: You saw this bar graph in Warmup. Now let's learn how to read it exactly.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Colors data. Red bar highlighted with guideline to axis at 40. Axis labeled 0–60 in 10s.

params.description: Axis labels 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60 highlight sequentially from bottom to top.

beats.text: See these numbers along the side? They show the scale. 10, 20, 30, 40. That's counting by 10s.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Red bar highlighted with guideline to axis at 40. Axis labels highlighted showing scale of 10.

beats.text: Red has 40. The height of the bar ends at the 40 line.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Red bar highlighted with guideline to axis at 40.

params.description: Axis labels 10, 20, 30, 40 pulse sequentially as counting animation plays.

beats.text: I can count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40. You already know how to count by 10s.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Red bar highlighted at 40. Counting animation complete.

params.title: Favorite Colors
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Colors data. Red=40, Blue=30, Green=50, Yellow=20. Axis 0-60, interval 10.
Blue bar highlights (height 30, ends at 30 axis line).

beats.text: Look at Blue. The bar's height ends at a line on the axis. What value does this show?
What value does this show?
Right. 30. The bar ends at the 30 line. Same as 3 symbols on the picture graph. Each symbol shows 10.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Colors data. Blue bar highlighted at height 30.
elements.title: Favorite Colors

params.title: Favorite Colors
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Colors data. Red=40, Blue=30, Green=50, Yellow=20. Y-axis 0-60 by 10s.
Green bar highlights, height 50.
Axis lines glow sequentially: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50.

beats.text: Green shows 50. Let's count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Colors data. Green bar highlighted, height 50. Axis marks 10-50 glowing after sequential animation.

params.description: Yellow bar highlights, height 20. Axis available for reference.

beats.text: You already know how to count by 10s. That's exactly what we do with scale of 10. The fourth bar shows Yellow. Count by 10s. What value does the bar show?
What value does the bar show?
Right. 20. Two jumps of 10: 10, 20.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Colors data. Yellow bar highlighted, height 20. Axis 0-60 by 10s visible for reference.

params.description: Data table displays Favorite Recess Activities: Swings=30, Tag=50, Slides=40, Jump Rope=20.
Vertical bar graph appears in building mode. Scale of 10, axis 0–60. Tag=50, Slides=40, Jump Rope=20 pre-filled. Swings bar empty. Ghost gridlines at 10s visible. Snap-to-10 enabled.

beats.text: Now you create a bar. Swings has 30 votes. Set the HEIGHT for Swings to show 30.
Set the HEIGHT for Swings to show 30.

validator.description: Student set Swings bar height to 30

params.description: Swings bar confirms at height 30.

beats.text: Right. 10, 20, 30. The bar ends at 30 on the axis. 30 votes for Swings.

elements.description: Data table showing Favorite Recess Activities values.

params.description: Data table appears showing Books Read This Month. Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30. Horizontal layout.

Vertical bar graph in building mode. Books Read This Month. Scale of 10, axis 0–70. Three bars pre-filled: Fiction=40, Comics=20, Poetry=30. Nonfiction bar empty. Ghost gridlines at 10s visible. Snap-to-10 enabled.

beats.text: Nonfiction has 60 books. That's the biggest number. Set the bar for Nonfiction.
Set the bar for Nonfiction to show 60.

validator.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to 60, correct

params.description: Nonfiction bar confirms at height 60.

beats.text: Right. Nonfiction at 60. You found the 60 line and set the bar there.

elements.description: Data table showing Books Read This Month. Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30.
Vertical bar graph in building mode. Books Read This Month. All four bars complete: Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30. Scale of 10, axis 0–70.

params.title: Books Read This Month
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Books Read This Month. All four bars visible: Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30. Y-axis 0-70, scale of 10.

beats.text: Here's the completed graph. Use it to answer a question.
How many MORE books were Nonfiction than Comics?
How many MORE books were Nonfiction than Comics?

validator.description: Student selected 40, correct. Nonfiction 60 minus Comics 20 equals 40.

params.description: Nonfiction and Comics bars highlight to confirm comparison.

beats.text: Right. Nonfiction is 60, Comics is 20. The difference is 40.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Books Read This Month. Nonfiction and Comics bars highlighted. Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30.

params.title: Weekly Steps
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Weekly Steps data. Mon=35, Tue=45, Wed=50, Thu=25, Fri=40. Scale of 10, y-axis 0–60.

beats.text: You can read bars that end right at the lines. But what if a bar ends between the lines?

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Weekly Steps data with five bars. Scale of 10, y-axis 0–60.
elements.title: Weekly Steps

params.title: Stickers Collected
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Stickers Collected. Emma=30 at 30 line, Jake=25 between 20–30 lines, Mia=40 at 40 line, Leo=35 between 30–40 lines. Scale of 10, axis 0–50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible (lighter than 10s lines).
Jake's bar highlights.

beats.text: Look at Jake's bar. It doesn't end at a line. It ends between the lines.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Stickers Collected. Emma=30, Jake=25, Mia=40, Leo=35. Jake's bar highlighted. Scale of 10, axis 0–50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

beats.text: Remember half-symbols from scale of 2? Half of 2 was 1. Same idea here. Half of 10 is 5.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Stickers Collected. Emma=30, Jake=25, Mia=40, Leo=35. Jake's bar highlighted. Scale of 10, axis 0–50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

beats.text: Half of 10 is 5. So between 20 and 30, that's 20 plus 5, which equals 25. Jake has 25 stickers.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Stickers Collected. Emma=30, Jake=25, Mia=40, Leo=35. Jake's bar highlighted. Scale of 10, axis 0–50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

params.title: Stickers Collected
params.description: Vertical bar graph titled Stickers Collected. Four bars: Emma=30, Jake=25, Mia=40, Leo=35. Scale of 10, axis 0-50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.
Leo's bar highlights. Height 35, between 30 and 40 gridlines.

beats.text: Now look at Leo's bar. It's also between the lines. Leo's bar is between 30 and 40. What value does this show?
What value does Leo's bar show?
Right. 35. It's halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 is 35.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph titled Stickers Collected. Leo's bar highlighted at height 35. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

params.description: Data table appears showing Points Scored: Team A=45, Team B=30, Team C=25, Team D=50, Team E=15.

Vertical bar graph appears in building mode. Points Scored. Scale of 10, axis 0–60. Four bars pre-filled: Team B=30, Team C=25, Team D=50, Team E=15. Team A bar empty. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible. Snap-to-5 enabled.

beats.text: Team A scored 45 points. Set the bar for Team A at 45. That's halfway between 40 and 50.
Set the bar for Team A.

validator.description: Student set Team A bar to height 45, correct

params.description: Team A bar confirms at height 45.

beats.text: That's it. 45 sits right between the lines. Halfway from 40 to 50.

elements.description: Data table showing Points Scored values: Team A=45, Team B=30, Team C=25, Team D=50, Team E=15.

params.description: Data table appears showing Minutes of Reading: Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25.
Vertical bar graph appears in building mode. Scale of 10, axis 0–60. No bars pre-filled. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible. Snap-to-5 enabled.

beats.text: Some values are at the axis lines. Some are between. You decide where each bar goes. Try to complete the graph.
Complete the graph.

validator.description: All four bars correctly placed: Monday=40 (at line), Tuesday=35 (between), Wednesday=50 (at line), Thursday=25 (between)

beats.text: Right. Monday and Wednesday are at the axis lines. Tuesday and Thursday are between the lines. You figured out which is which.

elements.description: Data table showing Minutes of Reading: Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25.
Vertical bar graph in building mode, all four bars complete: Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25. Scale 10, axis 0–60, ghost gridlines at 5s.

params.title: Minutes of Reading
params.description: Vertical bar graph titled Minutes of Reading appears. Four bars: Monday=40 (at line), Tuesday=35 (between lines), Wednesday=50 (at line), Thursday=25 (between lines). Scale of 10, axis range 0-60.

beats.text: Use this graph to answer. How many TOTAL minutes of reading for Monday and Tuesday?
How many TOTAL minutes of reading for Monday and Tuesday?

validator.description: Student selected 75, correct total for Monday (40) plus Tuesday (35)

params.description: Monday and Tuesday bars highlight to confirm selection.

beats.text: That's right. 75 minutes total. Monday is 40, Tuesday is 35. 40 plus 35 is 75.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph titled Minutes of Reading. Monday and Tuesday bars highlighted. Four bars showing Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25.

params.title: Minutes of Reading
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Minutes of Reading data. Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25. X-axis labeled 0–60 in intervals of 10.
